route.name: The LA Observer
route.region: Angeles National Forest / San Gabriel Mountains / Los Angeles
route.description: Public GPX Los Angeles-area overnighter with steep paved climbs, dirt roads, singletrack/trail pieces, and urban/transit access.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Los Angeles-area overnighter with steep paved climbs, dirt roads, singletrack/trail pieces, and urban/transit access.
route.bestSeason: Cooler months are safer; July is blocked unless Angeles NF closures, heat, water, and camp status all clear.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Los Angeles-area overnighter with steep paved climbs, dirt roads, singletrack/trail pieces, and urban/transit access.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Red Box, Mount Wilson, and other route water points must be verified; carry substantial capacity in July.
resources.food: Use Los Angeles/foothill services before and after; do not assume reliable on-route food.
resources.camping: Mt. Lowe/Angeles NF camp status must be verified; current route-source notes flag Eaton Fire impacts and closure uncertainty.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote until Angeles NF closures and Mt. Lowe status are verified.
Use as a cooler-season local route candidate.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Current July 10-13 screening blocks gate confirmation. Keep as planning/reference only.
Eaton Fire Area Closure appears to close route-relevant Mount Wilson and Mount Lowe corridors through 2027-12-31.
Raw GPX must not be used until every segment is overlaid against the Eaton closure order.
Angeles NF High Fire Restrictions and active Los Angeles County fire context block next-weekend promotion.
requiredFixes: Resolve the July-window blocker or choose a different route.
Build a legal reroute avoiding all closed Eaton Fire roads/trails and verify it with Angeles NF map/order.
Confirm camping is in developed/legal sites only and complies with High Fire Restrictions.
Refresh Angeles NF alerts, CAL FIRE incidents, smoke/AQI, and heat forecast before reconsidering.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Angeles/Eaton Fire closures and July heat/water block next-weekend promotion until an official-open reroute is verified.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Angeles NF fire danger is High through 2026-12-31 with wood/charcoal/stove-fire restrictions outside developed recreation sites; CAL FIRE lists active Chloe Fire in Los Angeles County.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: The raw GPX lead overlaps the Mt Lowe/Mt Wilson/Red Box Angeles NF corridor, and official Eaton Fire Area Closure closes route-relevant Mt Lowe/Mt Wilson trail/road names through 2027.

days.name: The LA Observer segment 1

readinessEvidence.finding: Eaton Fire Area Closure remains effective through 2027-12-31 and closes Mount Wilson Toll Road, Sam Merrill Trail, Echo Mountain Trail, Mount Lowe West Trail, and Mount Lowe Trail East.
Angeles NF High Fire Restrictions are effective June 12 through December 31, 2026; CAL FIRE active incidents list Chloe Fire in Los Angeles County.
